perturbation.original_text: 1. Form of Sponsorship. During the term of this Agreement, Tickets shall be MP3.com's exclusive partner/source for sports, entertainment, and travel tickets, and MP3.com shall include a Tickets Portal on the "Music" page and the "Pop," "Rock" and "Alternative" genre pages on the Website. A "Portal" is defined as a web graphic with the dimensions not to exceed 125 x 125 pixels and 20Kb in size. The content of the Portal shall be supplied by Tickets and shall conform with reasonable technical and content specifications supplied by MP3.com.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Form of Sponsorship. During the term of this Agreement, Tickets shall be MP3.com's exclusive partner/source for sports, entertainment, and travel tickets; however, MP3.com retains the right to partner with other ticket vendors for events outside of sports, entertainment and travel, and MP3.com shall include a Tickets Portal on the "Music" page and the "Pop," "Rock" and "Alternative" genre pages on the Website. A "Portal" is defined as a web graphic with the dimensions not to exceed 125 x 125 pixels and 20Kb in size. The content of the Portal shall be supplied by Tickets and shall conform with reasonable technical and content specifications supplied by MP3.com.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Tickets is MP3.com's 'exclusive partner/source' for tickets. The modified text adds that MP3.com can partner with other ticket vendors for events outside sports, entertainment, and travel, creating a contradiction regarding exclusivity. This undermines the original intent of exclusivity and can result in an in-text contradiction in the 'Form of Sponsorship' section, where Tickets is originally stated to be the exclusive partner.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Form of Sponsorship. During the term of this Agreement, Tickets shall be MP3.com's exclusive partner/source for sports, entertainment, and travel tickets; however, MP3.com retains the right to partner with other ticket vendors for events outside of sports, entertainment and travel, and MP3.com shall include a Tickets Portal on the "Music" page and the "Pop," "Rock" and "Alternative" genre pages on the Website. A "Portal" is defined as a web graphic with the dimensions not to exceed 125 x 125 pixels and 20Kb in size. The content of the Portal shall be supplied by Tickets and shall conform with reasonable technical and content specifications supplied by MP3.com.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Tickets is MP3.com's exclusive partner for sports, entertainment, and travel tickets. The changed text, which is also the contradicted text, introduces a caveat allowing MP3.com to partner with other vendors for events outside those categories. This directly contradicts the initial claim of exclusivity, creating an in-text contradiction because 'exclusive' means that Tickets should be the ONLY partner/source in those specific categories, which the changed text violates.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Term and Termination. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall remain in full force and effect until one (1) year subsequent to the Effective Date, provided however, that Tickets may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon thirty (30) days' notice to MP3. com at any time prior to the expiration of sixty (60) days subsequent to the Effective Date. Furthermore, for a thirty (30) day period, beginning thirty (30) days prior to the first anniversary of this Agreement, Tickets shall have the right to renew the Agreement for another year with Sponsor Fees that do not exceed a [***] percent increase over the existing Sponsor Fees. Any payments which have accrued prior to the date of termination shall remain due and payable. Sections 6, 7, and 8 shall survive termination of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Term and Termination. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall remain in full force and effect until one (1) year subsequent to the Effective Date. Furthermore, for a thirty (30) day period, beginning thirty (30) days prior to the first anniversary of this Agreement, Tickets shall have the right to renew the Agreement for another year with Sponsor Fees that do not exceed a [***] percent increase over the existing Sponsor Fees. MP3.com can terminate the agreement at any time, provided there is a major change in the market conditions that effects its business. Any payments which have accrued prior to the date of termination shall remain due and payable. Sections 6, 7, and 8 shall survive termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows Tickets to terminate the agreement within 60 days for any reason. The modification introduces that MP3.com can terminate agreement due to market conditions. It created an in-text contradiction in the 'Term and Termination' section. Because one part talks about Tickets and another talks about MP3.com
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Term and Termination. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall remain in full force and effect until one (1) year subsequent to the Effective Date. Furthermore, for a thirty (30) day period, beginning thirty (30) days prior to the first anniversary of this Agreement, Tickets shall have the right to renew the Agreement for another year with Sponsor Fees that do not exceed a [***] percent increase over the existing Sponsor Fees. MP3.com can terminate the agreement at any time, provided there is a major change in the market conditions that effects its business. Any payments which have accrued prior to the date of termination shall remain due and payable. Sections 6, 7, and 8 shall survive termination of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted Tickets the right to terminate the agreement for any reason within a specified timeframe. The changed text adds a clause allowing MP3.com to terminate the agreement under specific market conditions. This introduces a contradiction because it implies MP3.com's termination rights are conditional, while Tickets' termination rights (as stated in the contradicted section) are unconditional within the initial period. The new term in the changed text directly contradicts the previous terms, creating ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the termination rights of both parties as well as contradicting the original terms and creating a new contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Measurement. Upon request, Tickets shall have access to pertinent statistics related to Impressions covering the period of this contract. Tickets agrees to accept MP3.com's measurement of Impressions (the "Count") according to MP3.com's logs and other tracking devices and/or software MP3.com may use, provided however, that Tickets shall have the right to audit MP3.com's records in this regard. If Tickets reasonably disputes the Count pursuant to this Agreement, then Tickets shall have the right to select the independent auditor of its choice to conduct an audit of MP3.com's records (the "Audit"). The Audit will be conducted in such a way so as not to interfere to any material extent with MP3.com's operations. If, for any applicable period, the independent auditor determines that MP3.com overstated the Count by more than five percent (5%), than MP3.com shall pay the cost of the Audit and shall refund Tickets the difference between the amount originally paid and the amount which should have been paid, or MP3.com shall credit the appropriate amount of Impressions to Tickets' account.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Measurement. Upon request, Tickets shall have access to pertinent statistics related to Impressions covering the period of this contract. Tickets agrees to accept MP3.com's measurement of Impressions (the "Count") according to MP3.com's logs and other tracking devices and/or software MP3.com may use. MP3.com reserves the right to make adjustments to the Count at its sole discretion, and Tickets' audit rights are limited to verifying the methodology used by MP3.com, not the final Count. If Tickets reasonably disputes the methodology, then Tickets shall have the right to select the independent auditor of its choice to conduct an audit of MP3.com's methodology(the "Audit"). The Audit will be conducted in such a way so as not to interfere to any material extent with MP3.com's operations. If, for any applicable period, the independent auditor determines that MP3.com overstated the Count by more than five percent (5%), than MP3.com shall pay the cost of the Audit and shall refund Tickets the difference between the amount originally paid and the amount which should have been paid, or MP3.com shall credit the appropriate amount of Impressions to Tickets' account.
perturbation.explanation: The original text gives Tickets the right to audit MP3.com's records for accuracy. The modified text introduces that MP3.com can adjust the Count and limits Tickets' audit rights to methodology only. This creates a contradiction. In the beginning, Tickets has the right to audit the RECORDS but later that audit only extends to the METHODOLOGY.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Measurement. Upon request, Tickets shall have access to pertinent statistics related to Impressions covering the period of this contract. Tickets agrees to accept MP3.com's measurement of Impressions (the "Count") according to MP3.com's logs and other tracking devices and/or software MP3.com may use. MP3.com reserves the right to make adjustments to the Count at its sole discretion, and Tickets' audit rights are limited to verifying the methodology used by MP3.com, not the final Count. If Tickets reasonably disputes the methodology, then Tickets shall have the right to select the independent auditor of its choice to conduct an audit of MP3.com's methodology(the "Audit"). The Audit will be conducted in such a way so as not to interfere to any material extent with MP3.com's operations. If, for any applicable period, the independent auditor determines that MP3.com overstated the Count by more than five percent (5%), than MP3.com shall pay the cost of the Audit and shall refund Tickets the difference between the amount originally paid and the amount which should have been paid, or MP3.com shall credit the appropriate amount of Impressions to Tickets' account.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5 granted Tickets the right to audit MP3.com's records related to Impressions. The changed text in Section 5 significantly limits this right, stating that "Tickets' audit rights are limited to verifying the methodology used by MP3.com, not the final Count." This creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted section implicitly relies on the ability to audit the 'Count' (actual records) for accuracy, which is now explicitly prohibited. This directly contradicts the initial premise of being able to audit MP3.com's records to verify the Count, making the term unenforceable.
